channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: A Reliability Evaluation of Hybrid Deterministic-LLM Based Approaches for Academic Course Registration PDF Information Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2604.00003v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study evaluates the reliability of information extraction approaches from KRS documents using three strategies: LLM only, Hybrid Deterministic - LLM (regex + LLM), and a Camelot based pipeline with LLM fallback. Experiments were conducted on 140 ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, study, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Trustworthy Are LLM-as-Judge Ratings for Interpretive Responses? Implications for Qualitative Research Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2604.00008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As qualitative researchers show growing interest in using automated tools to support interpretive analysis, a large language model (LLM) is often introduced into an analytic workflow as is, without systematic evaluation of interpretive quality or comp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, large language model, study, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Eyla: Toward an Identity-Anchored LLM Architecture with Integrated Biological Priors -- Vision, Implementation Attempt, and Lessons from AI-Assisted Development
item.description: arXiv:2604.00009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the design rationale, implementation attempt, and failure analysis of Eyla, a proposed identity-anchored LLM architecture that integrates biologically-inspired subsystems -- including HiPPO-initialized state-space models, zero-initialized a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, model, vision, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can LLMs Perceive Time? An Empirical Investigation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models cannot estimate how long their own tasks take. We investigate this limitation through four experiments across 68 tasks and four model families. Pre-task estimates overshoot actual duration by 4--7$\times$ ($p < 0.001$), with mode...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, large language model, GPT, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Finding and Reactivating Post-Trained LLMs' Hidden Safety Mechanisms
item.description: arXiv:2604.00012v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the impressive performance of general-purpose large language models (LLMs), they often require fine-tuning or post-training to excel at specific tasks. For instance, large reasoning models (LRMs), such as the DeepSeek-R1 series, demonstrate st...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, fine-tuning, model, experiment, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
item.description: arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and agents. Existing emotion-aware studies mainly treat em...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, reasoning, large language model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Safety-Aware Role-Orchestrated Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Behavioral Health Communication Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Single-agent large language model (LLM) systems struggle to simultaneously support diverse conversational functions and maintain safety in behavioral health communication. We propose a safety-aware, role-orchestrated multi-agent LLM framework designed...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, prompt, framework, large language model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PASM: Population Adaptive Symbolic Mixture-of-Experts Model for Cross-location Hurricane Evacuation Decision Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2604.00074v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate prediction of evacuation behavior is critical for disaster preparedness, yet models trained in one region often fail elsewhere. Using a multi-state hurricane evacuation survey, we show this failure goes beyond feature distribution shift: hous...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, GPT, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: In harmony with gpt-oss
item.description: arXiv:2604.00362v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: No one has independently reproduced OpenAI's published scores for gpt-oss-20b with tools, because the original paper discloses neither the tools nor the agent harness. We reverse-engineered the model's in-distribution tools: when prompted without tool...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, model, prompt, paper, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Task-Centric Personalized Federated Fine-Tuning of Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.00050v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a promising technique for training language models on distributed and private datasets of diverse tasks. However, aggregating models trained on heterogeneous tasks often degrades the overall performance of indivi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Perspective: Towards sustainable exploration of chemical spaces with machine learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.00069v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence is transforming molecular and materials science, but its growing computational and data demands raise critical sustainability challenges. In this Perspective, we examine resource considerations across the AI-driven discovery pi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, research, API, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evolution Strategies for Deep RL pretraining
item.description: arXiv:2604.00066v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although Deep Reinforcement Learning has proven highly effective for complex decision-making problems, it demands significant computational resources and careful parameter adjustment in order to develop successful strategies. Evolution strategies offe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Speeding Up Mixed-Integer Programming Solvers with Sparse Learning for Branching
item.description: arXiv:2604.00094v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning is increasingly used to improve decisions within branch-and-bound algorithms for mixed-integer programming. Many existing approaches rely on deep learning, which often requires very large training datasets and substantial computationa...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
